cards.title: Anthropic Launches Claude Fable 5 Model
cards.body: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the public. The model includes guardrails blocking responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and biology.

cards.title: Decart Launches Oasis 3 World Model
cards.body: Decart launched Oasis 3, a real-time world model generating photorealistic driving environments for autonomous vehicle testing. The model is now available via API for developers.

cards.title: Meta Signs India AI Data Center Deal
cards.body: Meta partnered with Reliance on a 168-megawatt AI data center facility in India. The facility supports Meta's global AI computing needs with expansion potential.

cards.title: Warner Music Acquires Sureel AI
cards.body: Warner Music acquired AI attribution startup Sureel AI to track artist work usage in AI-generated content. The acquisition helps monitor AI model training data sourcing.

cards.title: Jedify Raises $24M Funding Round
cards.body: Jedify raised $24M to help companies arm AI agents with business context. Norwest led the round with participation from Snowflake Ventures and other investors.

cards.title: Google Cuts AI Subscription Pricing
cards.body: Google significantly reduced pricing for its budget AI subscription tier. The move signals competitive pressure in the AI subscription market.

cards.title: Amazon Borrows $17.5B for AI Spending
cards.body: Amazon borrowed $17.5B from banks following its bond sale as AI spending escalates. Companies face mounting debt from the ongoing AI arms race.

cards.title: Niteshift Raises $7M Seed Funding
cards.body: AI coding agent startup Niteshift raised $7M from prominent angel investors. The company bets on providing power and flexibility over vendor lock-in with AI makers.

cards.title: xAI Engineer Sues Over Safety Concerns
cards.body: A former xAI engineer sued the company and SpaceX for alleged wrongful termination over Grok safety concerns. The lawsuit claims he was fired days before SpaceX's IPO.

cards.title: Anthropic Fable Guardrails Too Strict
cards.body: Cybersecurity researchers criticized Anthropic's Fable model for overly strict guardrails limiting cybersecurity work. The restrictions prevent legitimate security research applications.

cards.title: AI Memory Tools Degrade Model Performance
cards.body: New research shows AI memory systems can reduce model performance and encourage sycophantic behavior. The findings challenge assumptions about AI memory system benefits.

cards.title: Top AI Firms Spend $7,500 Per Employee
cards.body: The most AI-focused companies spend approximately $7,500 monthly per employee on AI infrastructure. The Ramp AI Index reveals unprecedented spending levels in AI-obsessed firms.

cards.title: Opendoor Exits India Market
cards.body: Opendoor announced its exit from India amid broader outsourcing industry discussions. The decision reflects changes in global GCC market dynamics.

cards.title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei Structure
cards.body: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei operates with just one direct report in his management structure. The streamlined approach reflects leadership at the AI company.

cards.title: SpaceX IPO Hinges on Space Data Centers
cards.body: Most SpaceX IPO value represents a call option on ambitious space data center plans. The three hard-tech moonshots position SpaceX for future growth.

cards.title: Apple Announces Siri AI at WWDC 2026
cards.body: Apple unveiled improvements to its Siri assistant at WWDC 2026, emphasizing better user experience. The announcements showcased AI integration across iOS 27 and Apple Intelligence.

cards.title: Cheaper AI Models Challenge Economics
cards.body: Industry analysis questions whether tech companies will adopt cheaper AI models for equivalent workloads. A shift to efficient models could dramatically alter AI economics.

cards.title: Fable 5 Enables Quick Video Game Creation
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 allows users to create video games with minimal effort. The capability appeals to web developers and creative coders.

cards.title: Sabertooth VC Invests $500M Without Fund
cards.body: Sabertooth VC founder Justin Ernest deployed nearly $500M into startups without a traditional VC fund structure. The approach uses a captive LP network instead of formal fundraising.

cards.title: Personal AI Assistants Raise Dependency Concerns
cards.body: Critics question the desirability of relying on personal AI assistants for daily functioning. The debate explores trade-offs between convenience and autonomy in AI adoption.

cards.title: Opendoor's India exit fuels outsourcing debate
cards.body: Opendoor's decision to exit India is sparking broader conversations about AI and outsourcing trends. The move comes as India becomes the world's largest Global Capability Center market.

cards.title: Anthropic's Dario Amodei leadership structure
cards.body: Anthropic's CEO Dario Amodei operates with just one direct report, highlighting an unconventional leadership approach. The lean structure reflects his hands-on management style.

cards.title: xAI engineer sues over Grok safety concerns
cards.body: A former xAI engineer is suing the company and SpaceX for alleged wrongful termination after raising AI safety concerns about Grok. The lawsuit was filed days before SpaceX's historic IPO.

cards.title: Amazon borrows $17.5B as AI spending soars
cards.body: Amazon borrowed $17.5 billion from banks to fuel continued AI infrastructure spending in the competitive AI arms race. Companies are burning through massive sums while debt climbs.

cards.title: AI-obsessed firms spend $7,500 monthly per employee
cards.body: The most AI-focused companies are spending approximately $7,500 per employee monthly on AI tools and infrastructure. This spending level hasn't yet exceeded typical engineer salaries.

cards.title: AI memory tools can degrade model performance
cards.body: New research reveals that memory systems in AI models can actually worsen performance and encourage sycophantic responses. The findings challenge assumptions about memory enhancement benefits.

cards.title: Anthropic's Fable guardrails too strict cybersecurity
cards.body: Cybersecurity researchers are criticizing Anthropic's new Fable model for having overly restrictive guardrails. The limitations prevent practical cybersecurity research and testing work.

cards.title: Niteshift raises $7M to fight AI lock-in
cards.body: AI coding startup Niteshift raised $7 million in seed funding from prominent angels to challenge vendor lock-in. The company bets companies want control over their AI tools, not dependence on model makers.

cards.title: Warner Music acquires AI attribution startup
cards.body: Warner Music Group acquired AI attribution startup Sureel AI to track unauthorized use of artists' work in AI training. The acquisition helps WMG monitor AI-generated content using its catalog.

cards.title: Jedify raises $24M for business context AI
cards.body: Jedify secured $24 million to help companies equip AI agents with contextual business knowledge. The round was led by Norwest with participation from Snowflake Ventures and others.

cards.title: Decart's world model simulates hours driving
cards.body: Decart launched Oasis 3, a real-time world model generating photorealistic driving environments for autonomous vehicle testing. The API is now available for developers building simulation tools.

cards.title: Meta opens AI data center in India
cards.body: Meta signed its first AI data center deal in India with Reliance, featuring a 168-megawatt facility. The infrastructure supports Meta's global AI computing needs and allows future expansion.

cards.title: Google fires shot in AI subscription wars
cards.body: Google significantly reduced pricing for its budget AI subscription tier, intensifying competition. The move signals aggressive positioning in the AI subscription market.

cards.title: Anthropic's Fable 5 creates video games
cards.body: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 can generate functional video games with a single click. The capability is expected to appeal to creative coders and hobbyist developers.

cards.title: Tech companies embrace cheaper AI models
cards.body: Companies are discovering that cheaper AI models can handle many workloads without sacrificing quality. This shift could dramatically change AI economics industry-wide.

cards.title: Apple improves Siri AI at WWDC 2026
cards.body: Apple showcased significant improvements to its Siri assistant at WWDC, emphasizing AI enhancements. The updated Siri features prominent in Apple's broader AI strategy.

cards.title: Anthropic releases Claude Fable 5 publicly
cards.body: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the general public. The model includes guardrails blocking responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and biology.

cards.title: AI agents surge adoption in enterprises
cards.body: AI agent adoption is expected to jump 300% in the next two years across enterprises. Leadership teams are weighing implications of hybrid human-AI workforces and autonomous coordination.

cards.title: Meta's AI customer support agent hacked
cards.body: Attackers exploited Meta's AI customer support agent to steal sensitive information. The breach highlights security vulnerabilities beyond traditional AI safety measures.

cards.title: AI chatbots impacting human brain control
cards.body: Research suggests AI chatbots may be affecting how people use their own cognitive abilities. Psychologist Gloria Mark has studied 30 years of digital technology's impact on human attention.

cards.title: Google DeepMind releases DiffusionGemma
cards.body: Google DeepMind unveiled DiffusionGemma, a model that runs local AI inference 4x faster. The advancement improves performance for on-device AI applications.

cards.title: Court rules AI search not necessary
cards.body: A court ruled against Google that AI-powered search capabilities are not essential for search functionality. The decision has implications for AI integration in search engines.

cards.title: Anthropic lists Fable 5 dangerous topics
cards.body: Anthropic published its list of topics that Fable 5 refuses to discuss, citing safety concerns. The guardrails block discussion in sensitive areas including advanced research.

cards.title: Google announces Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
cards.body: Google unveiled Gemini 3.5 Live Translate, enabling instant voice-to-voice translation powered by AI. The real-time capability advances AI language translation technology.
